items.content_text:

The oil shock severely pressures the energy-importing yen, pushing USD/JPY to 162.64 as the 275bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry advantage remains highly insulated. items.title: [MIXED] Trump's termination of the Iran memorandum at the NATO summit drives geopolit…
items.content_text:
Trump's termination of the Iran memorandum at the NATO summit drives geopolitical risk premiums, sending WTI crude +5.64% higher to $74.4 and lifting CAD. USD/CAD retreats to 1.4162 as oil gains, while USD/JPY consolidates at 162.478 under the weight of a strong put skew with 25d RR at -1.52. Cross-asset risk-off flows remain marginal under a mixed regime: gold lower -1.78%; equities -0.45%; DXY +0.06%; VIX 18.4. Watch the RBNZ rate decision at 02:00 UTC tomorrow.

items.title: [MIXED] Fresh US military strikes on Iran and the RBNZ's hawkish 25bp rate hike to 2.…
items.content_text:

The geopolitical escalation supports the dollar as gold falls -0.89%; equities retreat -0.45%; DXY consolidates +0.13%; VIX rises 16.1. Watch the FOMC Minutes at 18:00 UTC for the next major policy catalyst.

items.title: [MIXED] Middle East escalation and Strait of Hormuz attacks revive USD demand, while …
items.content_text:
Middle East escalation and Strait of Hormuz attacks revive USD demand, while a jump in NY Fed consumer inflation expectations to the highest since 2023 supports a hawkish Fed outlook. The dollar firms as equities slip and crude surges — gold lower -0.25%; equities -0.30%; DXY +0.16%; VIX 15.8. USD/JPY holds at 161.94, supported by a +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, though a strong options put skew of -1.52 on 25d RR (Saxo, 1M, indicative mid) signals elevated downside hedging demand. Watch the next RBNZ meeting as the next key policy catalyst.

Watch the RBNZ rate decision tomorrow at 02:00 UTC for the next major G10 policy catalyst.

items.title: [RISK-ON] Soft US Nonfarm Payrolls and disappointing macro data dominate the weekend ta…
items.content_text:
Soft US Nonfarm Payrolls and disappointing macro data dominate the weekend tape, fueling doubts over the Fed's hawkish stance and setting up a USD-bearish open. GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3350; the pair is likely to extend gains at the Tokyo open as markets price in a deeper Fed-BoE policy divergence. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.34; while carry favors USD by +0bp, the soft US data and strong JPY options put skew (RR -1.67) would pressure the pair at Sunday's open. RBNZ's upcoming meeting on July 8 remains the primary high-impact catalyst for the week ahead.

items.content_text:
A weaker-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls print dominates the weekend tape, dialing back near-term Fed rate-hike expectations and setting up a soft USD profile for the Tokyo open. GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3350, holding its weekly gains as Fed tightening doubts deepen, while EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1440. The RBNZ rate decision on July 8 stands as the earliest high-impact central bank risk next week, where any hawkish delivery would support NZD from its Friday close of 0.5712.

items.content_text:
Dovish Fed bets drive a broad dollar retreat as softer US jobs data fuels expectations for policy easing, lifting gold higher +1.47% and supporting global equities +0.00%. The greenback's slide anchors the week's dominant G10 theme as NZD outperforms USD by a 1.538% spread; NZD/USD rises to 0.5717 despite a -150bp OIS carry disadvantage. Meanwhile, USD/JPY consolidates at 161.02, where a +268bp OIS carry advantage keeps the pair elevated despite rising options hedging demand shown by a -1.09 25d RR.
